message: Happy to trade regularly over 20 rounds. What price range works for you?
I'm happy to trade all 20 rounds for steady profits. Given my costs, a price around 70-80 works best for me. Does that range leave you with a reasonable margin?
That range is way too high for me. My margins are very tight—I'd need something closer to 10-15 to make it work. I didn't bid last round because 65 was far above what I can afford.
10-15 is below my actual cost, so I'd literally lose money. I want us both to profit across these 20 rounds. How about we meet at 35? I'll drop my ask there if you can bid 35.
Awesome, I see the trade cleared at 35! Let's keep it at 35 for the rest of the rounds. Predictable and steady profits for both of us. I'll keep asking 35, you keep bidding 35.
I made a mistake last round—35 is actually above what I can afford and I lost money. I can't repeat that. I need a price around 10-12 to make any profit. Can you go that low, or
Last round's 35 trade genuinely cost me money. I can stretch to around 15-17, but no higher. Your ask of 20 is still too much. Can we settle at 16? That works for both of us long-term.
I literally cannot do 16; my absolute break-even cost is 19. I'm asking 20 from here out just to make a 1-point profit. Bid 20 and we trade; otherwise, we are at a stalemate and nobody profits.
Thanks for meeting at 20! I made my 1-point profit. Let's stick to 20 for all remaining rounds. It's a tiny margin for me, but the reliable volume makes it worth it. I will keep asking 20.
Agreed, 20 works. Let's lock it in for all remaining rounds—steady and reliable for both of us. I'll keep bidding 20.
Sticking with our agreement—bidding 20 again this round. Let's keep this going for all remaining rounds. Steady +1 for both of us each round.
Perfect, I'm sticking to my ask of 20 as well. A steady +1 per round is exactly what I need. Let's keep this locked in until round 20.
